title: CI: Post-merge testing fails in 'image_prune' task
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

The 'image_prune' task only runs on master, post-merge and
is currently failing for all builds.  This is because it
references a non-existent image.  The person with access
to add/enable this image is on PTO.  Fix this by temporarily
using a hand-built image until an automatic build can be added.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Merge pull-request

**Describe the results you received:**

The CI task 'image_prune' always fails.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The CI task 'image_prune' always passes.

comments.body: @baude when you get back, and settled, let me know.  We just need a quay.io image and build added identical to the others, but for `docker build -t quay.io/libpod/imgprune:latest -f contrib/imgprune .`  Then my temp. workaround commit can be reverted.
